exemplar.title:
To what extent did the lack of decisive international intervention contribute to the
escalation of the humanitarian crisis during the Tigray War Between 2020-2022 In Ethiopia?

detailedExemplarData.title:
To what extent did the lack of decisive international intervention contribute to the
escalation of the humanitarian crisis during the Tigray War Between 2020-2022 In Ethiopia?

strengths: Topic is communicated accurately and effectively with comprehensive contextual framing
Research question is clearly stated, sharply focused, and consistently linked to discussion
Methodology identifies a broad and relevant range of sources and justifies theoretical lenses
weaknesses: Methodology explanation remains somewhat generic without detailed procedures
Occasional repetition in explanation of key concepts
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student meets the 5â6 descriptor in two strands (communication and research question) and shows mostly complete methodology. Occasional lapses in precision and a generic treatment of methods prevent full marks, placing this criterion securely at 5.

strengths: Clear and coherent knowledge of the Tigray conflict chronology and key actors
Application of source material is generally appropriate and supports the argument
Terminology is accurate and used consistently throughout
weaknesses: Heavy reliance on newsâbased sources over peerâreviewed literature
Superficial interrogation of source biases and comparative perspectives
Several citation errors and missing details in statistics
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While one strand sits at the middle band, the other two clearly fulfil the 5â6 descriptor through effective use of sources and consistent disciplinary understanding. This justifies a high-end band score of 5.

strengths: Research draws on a broad range of relevant secondary sources and comparative cases
Analysis is structured around clear thematic factors with causeâeffect reasoning
Argument integrates counterâpoints and offers a reasoned evaluation of intervention outcomes
weaknesses: Limited use of primary documents and some overreliance on think-tank journalism
Evaluation occasionally remains descriptive rather than deeply critical
Some comparative analogies are asserted without systematic synthesis
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work demonstrates a coherent, wellâsupported argument and systematic evaluation consistent with the top of the 4â6 band. Despite gaps in primaryâsource depth, the overall quality of reasoning and conclusion support a maximum band score of 6.

strengths: Clear and logical structure with appropriately formatted headings
Layout considerations (title page, section breaks) support reader navigation
Consistent presentation of tables and figures enhances clarity
weaknesses: Inconsistent citation style and missing page numbers in the table of contents
Typographical and minor formatting errors (e.g., incorrect header labels)
Some abrupt transitions and truncated sentences disrupt flow
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The presentation fully meets the 3â4 band descriptors in both structure and layout. Minor formatting and typographical lapses are present but do not impede understanding, warranting the top score of 4.

strandSelections.reasoning:
The essay moves beyond description by linking realist, liberal and neo-colonial lenses to concrete events (e.g., how sovereignty limited mediation).  Some causeâeffect reasoning is offered, and counter-points (e.g., noting AU/U.S. sanctions that *did* help) provide balance.  

Nevertheless, several sections drift into narrative listing of atrocities with limited analytical depth; comparative examples are asserted rather than systematically analysed.  Conclusions are partially supported but occasionally rely on broad generalisations.  

This places the analysis solidly within the âadequate/goodâ 4â6 band, but nearer the lower end, warranting a 4.

The student consults a reasonable breadth of sources (UNHCR, CFR, Brookings, academic PDFs) and links them to sub-arguments, demonstrating research that is âmostly relevantâ to the RQ. Comparative cases (Darfur, Rohingya, Libya) show an attempt to triangulate.  

However, reliance on journalism and think-tank pieces limits depth; primary documents (UNSC minutes, AU communiquÃ©s) are absent.  These weaknesses keep the work in the 4â6 band rather than the top two bands.  

Given the breadth and relative relevance but only moderate depth, a mid-band score of 5 is appropriate.

The essay follows a logical progression: Introduction, Methodology, themed subsections, Conclusion, References.  Headings aid navigation, and the reader can track the argument.  Minor flawsâmisspelled âTable of Contact,â inconsistent numbering and occasional abrupt transitionsâimpede flow but do not seriously hinder understanding.  

Overall, the structure supports comprehension, fulfilling the 3â4 band at a score of 3.

The essay shows clear knowledge of the Tigray conflict chronology, actors (TPLF, Eritrean army, AU, UN) and humanitarian outcomes.  Global-politics disciplinary understanding is demonstrated through repeated reference to theories and key concepts (sovereignty, R2P, Jus in Bello).  

Source use is partially effectiveâquotations and data points are inserted appropriately, yet deeper engagement with academic debate (e.g., competing interpretations of R2P efficacy) is limited.  Some factual oversimplifications (e.g., stating AU âdid nothing for ten monthsâ without nuance) reduce coherence.

Overall, knowledge is clear and generally coherent, fulfilling the 3â4 descriptor at the higher end.

The student presents a structured argument (five thematic factors) and attempts evaluation by referencing competing theories and acknowledging limited successes of sanctions/mediation.  Yet evaluation is often brief and lacks methodological reflection on alternative explanations (e.g., internal Ethiopian politics).  The conclusion largely restates earlier points without synthesising them.  

Therefore, the work fits the descriptor of âreasoned argument with partial critical evaluation,â meriting a 4 in the 4â6 band.

Formatting elements are mostly in place: title page shows word count and research topic; section headings are bold; references are listed.  Page numbers are not shown in the excerpt, and citation style is inconsistent (mix of in-text parenthetical and narrative references).  

Nevertheless, basic layoutâclear paragraphs, headings, tablesâis correctly applied, meeting the expectations of the 3â4 band at the lower mark of 3.

Key political-science termsâstate sovereignty, Responsibility to Protect, liberalism, neo-colonialismâare used accurately and consistently.  The student correctly distinguishes pillars of R2P and contrasts realist vs. liberal expectations.  

A few minor inaccuracies occur (e.g., misspelling of âliberalismâ as *Liberalism* in places, or conflating realism explanations for both Darfur and Tigray without nuance), but these do not impede understanding.  

Hence the terminology usage meets the âmostly accurateâ description and warrants 4 within the 3â4 band.

The student states a clear, concise topic in the cover information and reiterates it in the introduction. Background on the Tigray conflict, the scale of the humanitarian crisis and the relevance of international intervention are all explained in sufficient depth, demonstrating accurate contextual framing.  

Key concepts such as humanitarian crisis, sovereignty and responsibility to protect are introduced early, allowing the reader to understand why the topic matters in global-politics terms. Explanations are generally comprehensive, though occasionally repetitive and lacking precision in statistics, preventing a full mark in the top band.

Overall, the topic is communicated accurately and effectively; minor lapses in precision prevent a 6, but the work comfortably meets the 5â6 descriptor.

The methodology paragraph identifies a broad range of secondary sourcesâIGOs, NGOs, news outlets, academic papersâand briefly explains criteria such as reliability, bias and timeliness.  The student also justifies the choice of theoretical lenses (realism, liberalism, constructivism, neo-colonialism), indicating some informed selection.  

However, the explanation remains generic (e.g., âbiases were mitigatedâ without describing methods) and no clear primary research or systematic data-collection procedure is outlined.  Citation style is inconsistent, signalling only partial methodological rigour.

This aligns with the 3â4 descriptor: methodology is mostly complete, sources are generally relevant, and there is someâbut not âclearââevidence of effective selection.

The research question is explicitly stated, narrowly time-bounded (2020-2022) and sharply focused on the variable of âdecisive international intervention.â  Throughout the essay, section headings (e.g., diplomatic engagement, delayed mediation) map directly onto elements of the question, showing a strong logical link between the RQ and the ensuing discussion.

Some sub-sections drift into descriptive narrative (e.g., detailed recounting of troop movements) which slightly blurs the sharpness of focus; however, the connection to the RQ is maintained overall.  

Given the clear formulation and strong alignment with the essay structure, the work merits the upper band, though not the full 6 because occasional digressions reduce the razor-sharp focus expected at the very top.

Source material is used throughout, with specific references to CFR, Amnesty, Brookings, BBC and academic articles.  The citations support factual claims (death tolls, displacement figures) and comparative cases (Darfur, Myanmar).  

Nevertheless, many references are news pieces or opinion columns rather than peer-reviewed studies, and integration is sometimes superficialâsources are quoted for facts but rarely interrogated for reliability or contrasted with one another.  Occasional citation errors and missing page numbers further weaken application.

Thus, while relevance is evident, appropriateness and depth are only âmostlyâ adequate, positioning the work at the middle-band score of 3.

annotations.comment: The final summary repeats earlier theory discussion; condense this section to avoid redundancy.
Section headings (Introduction, Methodology, Main Body) are clearly formatted, aiding reader navigation.
Conclusion introduces the phrase âhumanitarian aid using the Tigray,â which is unclear. Revise for clarity and accuracy.
The research question is clearly stated and sharply focused, with precise time bounds and direct relevance to the discussion.
The phrasing of the research topic is broad; consider specifying which organizations or types of intervention are under review to sharpen focus.
The header reads âTable of Contactâ instead of âContents.â Correct this to adhere to standard report conventions.
The table of contents lists sections without page numbers. Add page references for each entry to improve navigation.
Typographical error in date range â19991â2012â undermines credibility. It should read â1991â2012.â
Relying predominantly on newsâbased trackers limits disciplinary depth. Incorporate peerâreviewed journals to strengthen source validity.
Effective introduction of the research question at the end of the introduction provides clear direction for the essay.
Methodology description states âbiases were mitigatedâ without detailing how. Explain specific validation steps and crossâchecking procedures.
How precisely does the Darfur comparison illuminate diplomatic failures in Tigray? Consider tighter linkage to avoid superficial analogy.
Several sentences are truncated midâthought, disrupting flow. Ensure all paragraphs are complete and logically concluded.
Inconsistent italicization of ârealism theoryâ appears. Apply uniform formatting for all theoretical terms.
The paper claims AU mediation âended the conflictâ here but later describes only shortâterm success. Reconcile these contradictory evaluations.
Displacement figures (âover one millionâ) lack an inâtext citation. Provide precise sourcing and date for this statistic.
Claims about Chinaâs strategic investments need supporting data (e.g., project values, trade volumes). Add concrete evidence.
Theoretical term âLiberalismâ shifts between lowercase and italics. Ensure consistent presentation of discipline-specific terminology.
The Rohingya comparison is interesting but lacks depth; analyze how differing geopolitical contexts affected response levels.
Add specific dates or a timeline in the âDelayed Peace Mediationâ section to clarify when each mediation effort began.
Attribution of UNHCR data is ambiguous. Specify the exact UNHCR report and date to enhance the reliability of your figures.
Conflicting statements about the success of mediation require resolution. Clarify whether outcomes were short- or long-term.
The term ârealistâ is not formatted consistently. Italicize all IR theory labels for uniformity.
The Syria comparison is descriptive; structure the evaluation with clear criteria (e.g., timing, scale, institutional response).
Constructivism is invoked but not defined; include a concise theoretical definition early to guide your analysis.
Broaden evaluation of Eritrean involvement by discussing longer-term humanitarian and political consequences beyond atrocity counts.
Claimed starvation as punishment needs empirical backing; include victim counts or aid blockage statistics.
The Libya analogy is noted but not contrasted in outcome; integrate a comparative framework to deepen your synthesis.
The conclusion concisely revisits main arguments and ties them back to the research question.
The TPLF quote lacks page or paragraph locator; add this detail to adhere to citation standards.
Excellent adherence to IB formatting: word count is clearly stated under the word limit.
The title âGLOBAL POLITICS EXTENDED ESSAYâ is prominently placed and accurately labels the investigation.
Citation style fluctuates around Meressa Tsehaye Gebrewahd (2024). Standardize inâtext citations per chosen style guide.
References on this page show mixed punctuation and italics; apply consistent style (e.g., APA) with hanging indents.
Several reference entries lack retrieval dates or have broken URLs. Complete all details for full traceability.
Ensure each bibliography item is on its own line and not split; this will improve readability and accuracy.
Align hanging indents consistently across all entries to present a polished reference list.
